verdict.summary: colcon build or ros2 run cannot locate the package. Usually the workspace is not sourced or the package name is misspelled.

dead_ends.action: Re-run colcon build without sourcing the workspace first
dead_ends.why_fails: Building and running in the same terminal without sourcing install/setup.bash means the new package is invisible to ros2 CLI
System install paths are managed by apt; manual copies get overwritten and break dependency resolution

dead_ends.action: Add the package path to AMENT_PREFIX_PATH by hand
dead_ends.why_fails: Does not persist across terminals and masks the real issue: the workspace overlay is not sourced

workarounds.action: Source the workspace overlay after building
workarounds.how: cd ~/ros2_ws && colcon build --packages-select my_package && source install/setup.bash

workarounds.action: Verify package.xml <name> matches the directory name and CMakeLists project() name
Check that the package is listed in colcon list output
